blocks.content: Use this interactive guide to create a **usage alert** in the **Cost Management and Billing** app. You'll set a budget on your total Grafana Cloud spend and choose where Grafana sends the notification, so you hear about cost changes before they show up on an invoice.
Open the **Cost Management and Billing** app to manage your Grafana Cloud usage and spend.
Click the **Usage Alerts** tab to manage alerts on your Grafana Cloud usage and spend.
Click **Create usage alert** to open the alert builder. If you already have alerts, the same control is labeled **+ Create usage alert**.
Keep **Global usage** selected to alert on your total Grafana Cloud spend. You can also choose attributed stack usage or anomaly detection.
Leave **Product** set to **Global spend** for a budget alert. Pick a specific product instead to alert on that product's usage.
Enter your monthly budget in dollars, for example `1000`. Grafana alerts you as your spend approaches this amount.

Alert levels decide how close to the budget you get notified. Select one or more preset levels (for example 50%, 70%, 85%, and 95% of the threshold), or add a custom level to fine-tune when Grafana warns you.
Choose a **Contact point** for the notification, or create one if you don't have a contact point set up yet.
Review your settings, then click **Create alerts** to start monitoring. This guide stops here so it doesn't create a real alert on your stack.

That's it. You set a budget on your global spend, chose alert levels, and picked a contact point. Grafana now has everything it needs to warn you before your Grafana Cloud spend crosses the budget you set.
